guest_name: Louise Perry, Erica Komisar, Deborah Frances-White

key_insights.insight: Excessive freedom without structure, particularly for children, can lead to feelings of being untethered and insecure.
key_insights.quote: Excessive freedom without any structure at all can also make us feel untethered, unbound,without connections to the peoplethat we're most intimate with.

key_insights.insight: The pill gave the illusion that sex was consequence free, leading to emotional complexities, especially for women, who often bond more quickly.
key_insights.quote: What the pill did when it arrived on the scene in the late 60s was it gave the illusion that sex was consequence free.And it isn't on a physical level, not really.But I think just as importantly, it became no less emotionally consequential.

key_insights.insight: Young people are more vulnerable to the negative impacts of hookup culture (loneliness, depression, anxiety) because they are still developing emotionally and mentally.
key_insights.quote: They are more vulnerable emotionally to what the hookupculture does to them. There's a lot of research to say that it causes loneliness,depression, anxiety, tons of regret,embarrassment, shame, self-esteem issues.

key_insights.insight: Overemphasis on professional work and paid labor has denigrated the importance of raising emotionally, mentally, and physically healthy children.
key_insights.quote: We have shiftedthis narrative towards working out in the worldand paid labor and accomplishments out in the worldand achievements out in the world,as opposed to what's right in front of you,the accomplishment, which is maybe the greatest accomplishmentof raising emotionally,mentally, physically healthy children that you put out into the world for the next generation.

key_insights.insight: Both sexes often seek the privileges of traditional gender roles without the corresponding responsibilities.
key_insights.quote: Both sexes want the privilegeswithout the responsibilities.

key_insights.insight: Feminism, while providing agency, has also been used to justify government policies that prioritize GDP over family wellbeing, channeling women into the workforce.
key_insights.quote: Governments at about the turn of the millennium seem to have cottoned on to the fact that they can use women to boost GDP.

key_insights.insight: The sexual revolution has provided agency and autonomy for women but not enough emotional freedom.
key_insights.quote: The sexual revolution has given women in spades, agency, autonomy, and emotional freedom. We don't yet have enough emotional freedom.

key_insights.insight: Campaigns to liberalize laws around BDSM raise concerns about freedom for whom, especially in cases where violence results in death and the defense of consensual sex is used to avoid murder prosecution.
key_insights.quote: If you're going to say that sexual freedom is the preeminent value, then I don't know what we do in these cases...where it seems to me that a terrible wrong has been done to this woman. And if we don't have illiberal laws, at least partially illiberal laws, then men who make this defense are going to get away with it.

actionable_advice.advice: Balance freedom with structure when raising children to foster security and stability.
actionable_advice.how_to: Provide age-appropriate choices (e.g., breakfast options, clothing) while maintaining consistent routines and boundaries.

actionable_advice.advice: For those struggling with emptiness and loneliness amidst hookup culture, prioritize emotional connection in sexual encounters.
actionable_advice.how_to: Seek out partners who care about you and whom you care about, focusing on building intimacy and trust before engaging in sexual activity.

actionable_advice.advice: Actively promote or support companies that provides the oppurtunity to women to stay at home.
actionable_advice.how_to: Vote for more people or companies that supports programs to women the opportunity to be present at home for the first years for their children.

actionable_advice.advice: Teach boys to be empathetic and support them to compete with the world.
actionable_advice.how_to: Support young boys and look out for them. Do not tell the girls to step back, and make more space for boys.

actionable_advice.advice: Acknowledge women’s choices while not diminishing.
actionable_advice.how_to: Admire the choice to stay home with your children and admire our choice not to have children.

memorable_stories: Deborah's escape from a religious cult and her journey to autonomy and freedom.
Louise's experience working in a rape crisis center and seeing the limitations of feminist theory in real-world situations.
The anecdote about a night at a music festival resulting in a passionate encounter and Deborah's defensiveness against the suggestion that this minimizes rape.
Erica's husband's joke, once they had children and she worked less, that she'll take over the family revenue while he sits by the pool
contrarian_claims: The second wave of feminism has caused more harm then good.
Feminism has been co-opted by governments to boost GDP by forcing women into the workforce and removing state support.
Women are neurologically more fragile than girls. We should separate boys and girls in college, and have quotas for both sexes.
That in todays world, in some cases, men are emotionally less supported than women.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - THE FEMINISM DEBATE: Is Feminism Betraying Women? The Hidden Risk Of Casual Sex!
